qtype: ratio simplification, dividing in a ratio, percentage of quantity
tex:
The music teacher at a school forms an orchestra.
\newline
\\
The instruments in the orchestra are 36 string, 15 woodwind and 12 brass.
\newline

(a) Write the ratio string : woodwind : brass in its simplest form.
\newline

 :  : 

(b) The 36 string instruments are violins, cellos and double basses in the ratio

violins : cellos : double basses = 9 : 2 : 1.
\newline

(i) (c) Show that the number of violins is 27.
\newline

(d) Work out the number of cellos and the number of double basses.
\newline

Cellos 

Double basses 

(e) The 15 woodwind instruments are oboes, flutes and clarinets.
\newline
\\
20\% of these instruments are oboes.
\newline
\\
There are twice as many flutes as clarinets.
\newline

Find the number of flutes.
\newline

(f) The 12 brass instruments are trumpets, trombones and horns.
\newline
\\
$\dfrac{1}{3}$ of these instruments are trumpets, 3 are trombones and the remainder are horns.
\newline
\\
Find the number of horns.
\newline

(g) The music teacher needs to buy all the instruments for the orchestra.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|r|r|r|}
\hline
Number of & Price of each & Cost (\$) \\
instruments & instrument (\$) & \\
\hline
String & 36 & 131 & 4716 \\
Woodwind & 15 & 217 \\
Brass & 12 & 221 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (h) Complete the table.
\newline

(i) Find the total cost of all the instruments.
\newline

\$ 

(j) The school is given 65\% of the total cost of all the instruments.
\newline

Find how much more money is needed.
\newline

\$

content.content:
Write the ratio string : woodwind : brass in its simplest form.
\newline

The 36 string instruments are violins, cellos and double basses in the ratio

violins : cellos : double basses = 9 : 2 : 1.
\newline

Show that the number of violins is 27.
\newline

Work out the number of cellos and the number of double basses.
\newline

Cellos \AnswerLine[][]

Double basses \AnswerLine[][]

The 15 woodwind instruments are oboes, flutes and clarinets.
\newline
\\
20\% of these instruments are oboes.
\newline
\\
There are twice as many flutes as clarinets.
\newline

Find the number of flutes.
\newline

The 12 brass instruments are trumpets, trombones and horns.
\newline
\\
$\dfrac{1}{3}$ of these instruments are trumpets, 3 are trombones and the remainder are horns.
\newline
\\
Find the number of horns.
\newline

The music teacher needs to buy all the instruments for the orchestra.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|r|r|r|}
\hline
Number of & Price of each & Cost (\$) \\
instruments & instrument (\$) & \\
\hline
String & 36 & 131 & 4716 \\
Woodwind & 15 & 217 \\
Brass & 12 & 221 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Complete the table.
\newline

Find the total cost of all the instruments.
\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[][]

The school is given 65\% of the total cost of all the instruments.
\newline

Find how much more money is needed.
\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[][]

tex:

\hline

qtype: money calculations, time duration, currency exchange, percentage spending
tex:
Mr Razif travels by bus from Singapore to Kuala Lumpur with his wife and his four children.
\newline

(a) \begin{tabular}{|l|r|}
\hline
Ticket Price \\
\hline
Adult & \$32.40 \\
\hline
Child & \$24.40 \\
\hline
Family (2 adults and 3 children) & \$115.00 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Work out how much Mr Razif saves if he buys a family ticket and one child ticket rather than six individual tickets.
\newline

\$ 

(b) The bus leaves Singapore at 12 40 and arrives in Kuala Lumpur at 17 35.
\newline

Work out, in hours and minutes, the time this journey takes.
\newline

 h  min

(c) Mr Razif changes some dollars into Malaysian ringgits.
\newline
\\
He receives 318 ringgits when the exchange rate is \$1 = 4.24 ringgits.
\newline

Work out how many dollars he changes.
\newline

\$

content.content:
\begin{tabular}{|l|r|}
\hline
Ticket Price \\
\hline

\hline
Child & \$24.40 \\
\hline
Family (2 adults and 3 children) & \$115.00 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Work out how much Mr Razif saves if he buys a family ticket and one child ticket rather than six individual tickets.
\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[][]

\$ \AnswerLine[][]

_rawBody:
Mr Razif travels by bus from Singapore to Kuala Lumpur with his wife and his four children.

\begin{parts}
\item \begin{tabular}{|l|r|}
\hline
Ticket Price \\
\hline
Adult & \$32.40 \\
\hline
Child & \$24.40 \\
\hline
Family (2 adults and 3 children) & \$115.00 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Work out how much Mr Razif saves if he buys a family ticket and one child ticket rather than six individual tickets.

\$ \AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{4}

\item The bus leaves Singapore at 12 40 and arrives in Kuala Lumpur at 17 35.

Work out, in hours and minutes, the time this journey takes.

\AnswerLine[] h \AnswerLine[] min
\Marks{1}

\item Mr Razif changes some dollars into Malaysian ringgits.\\
He receives 318 ringgits when the exchange rate is \$1 = 4.24 ringgits.

Work out how many dollars he changes.

\$ \AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}
\end{parts}
